finding: Hero and FAQ block markers carry aria-label on an element whose role prohibits it, so the label is dropped and the blocks are unnamed to a screen reader.

evidence.quote: aria-prohibited-attr (serious): span[aria-label="Hero block"], span[aria-label="FAQ block"] (count 2)

persona_rationale: I navigate blocks by screen reader; an unnamed block marker means I can't tell where one block ends and the next begins.

suggested_fix.current: <span aria-label="Hero block"> on a role that prohibits aria-label
suggested_fix.proposed: Move the label to a focusable element with an allowed role, or use a visually-hidden text node: Hero block…

finding: The Readiness and Status columns convey page state by a bare colored dot alone, with no visible text or shape.

evidence.quote: READINESS and STATUS columns render as bare yellow/red/blue/green dots
cell "SEO readiness: Needs work": button "SEO readiness: Needs work"

persona_rationale: At 200% zoom with low color discrimination I cannot read the two most important status columns; the dot is invisible signal to me.

suggested_fix.proposed: Dot plus a short visible text label ("Needs work" / "Published") or distinct per-state shapes/icons

finding: The disabled Publish button gives no programmatically-associated reason; the blocker is small orange text and is stuffed into the button's accessible name rather than a navigable description.

evidence.quote: Greyed Publish button with small orange 'Fix SEO title' text
button "Publish status Published Improve SEO title"

persona_rationale: A screen reader announces the button name but I can't navigate to or enumerate the blockers; I don't know what to fix or where.

suggested_fix.current: Greyed button with blocker text crammed into its accessible name
suggested_fix.proposed: Keep button enabled and add aria-describedby pointing at a focusable, listed 'Why can't I publish?' region enumerating every blocker

finding: The SEO panel form fields are tab stops 28-33 of 50; publish/SEO controls sit behind the entire rendered page preview and footer nav in tab order.

evidence.quote: tabOrder: stop 28 input 'Page title', stop 33 textarea 'Meta description', stop 27 button 'Publish changes', stop 44 button 'Restore draft'

persona_rationale: Keyboard-only, I must tab through ~27 controls I don't want to reach the fields I edit on every single page.

suggested_fix.current: Single DOM order forcing ~27 stops before the SEO editing panel
suggested_fix.proposed: Make the SEO panel a top-level landmark early in tab order, or add a 'Skip to SEO settings' skip link

finding: The revision preview page has two nested main landmarks.

evidence.quote: landmark-no-duplicate-main, landmark-main-is-top-level, landmark-unique (moderate) on #main-content / #main-content > main

- main:
  - main:

persona_rationale: 'Jump to main content' is ambiguous with two mains; I land in the wrong region.

suggested_fix.proposed: Single <main>; the inner wrapper becomes a <div> or <section> with a heading

finding: The sidebar complementary landmark is nested inside main instead of being a top-level document landmark, across the list, redirects, and editor pages.

evidence.quote: landmark-complementary-is-top-level (moderate): aside (editor page)

- main:
  - complementary:
    - button "Collapse sidebar"

persona_rationale: Screen-reader landmark navigation is how I orient; the nav announcing as 'inside main content' breaks my mental map of the page.

suggested_fix.proposed: Move the sidebar <aside>/<nav> out of <main> to a top-level document landmark

finding: A nextjs-portal element receives keyboard focus with no visible focus indicator on every page, at multiple stops per page.

evidence.quote: tabIssues: 'no visible focus indicator' — /admin/pages stop 46; /admin/pages/new stops 6/14/22/30/38/46; revision 8 stops; redirects 3 stops; block-preview 5 stops; public 2 stops

persona_rationale: Keyboard-only, an invisible focus stop means my focus vanishes and I don't know where I am on the page.

suggested_fix.current: Focusable portal element with no outline
suggested_fix.proposed: Set tabindex="-1" on non-interactive portals, or apply a visible :focus-visible outline

finding: After saving a scheduled publish, the field re-renders empty and no 'Scheduled for…' status appears anywhere; no confirmation reaches a screen reader.

evidence.quote: Schedule field buried in Advanced SEO; no status text after save
the field re-rendered EMPTY and no "Scheduled for …" status appeared anywhere in the editor

persona_rationale: I rely on announced confirmations; with no status text I cannot tell if the schedule saved, failed, or cleared.

suggested_fix.proposed: Persist the value and render a role="status" line ('Scheduled to publish {date} {tz}') announced on save

finding: The destructive 'Archive page' item in the row menu is distinguished from non-destructive items by red text alone.

evidence.quote: Red 'Archive page' item among black 'Edit page' / 'Move to draft' items

persona_rationale: At 200% zoom with reduced color perception I can't tell the irreversible item from 'Move to draft' and may archive a live page by mistake.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a warning icon and keep the destructive item visually grouped/separated so destructiveness isn't color-dependent

finding: Field-level show/hide toggles are tiny icon-only eye buttons below a comfortable target size.

evidence.quote: Small eye icons next to Eyebrow / Body / CTA fields
button "Show eyebrow" / button "Show body" / button "Hide cta"

persona_rationale: Motor impairment makes small targets a real miss-and-retry cost on a control I use per field.

suggested_fix.proposed: Enlarge the hit area to >=24px (AAA 24x24 / touch 44x44) while keeping the existing accessible name

finding: The create-page wizard has duplicate, identically-named 'Create page' region landmarks nested inside each other.

evidence.quote: landmark-unique (moderate): .p-0

region "Create page":
    - region "Create page":

persona_rationale: Two identically-named regions in the landmark list give me no way to tell them apart when jumping.

suggested_fix.proposed: Make the inner wrapper a non-landmark <div>, keeping a single named region

finding: A publish-required field ('CTA destination URL') exists only inside the Block settings modal, and the publish blocker text never says where to find it.

evidence.quote: the destination field is NOT on the canvas; it lives in Block actions → Edit settings modal
CTA destination URL field only present inside the Block Settings modal

persona_rationale: A screen-reader user gets a blocker naming a field that isn't in the reading order; I'd never find it without sighted trial-and-error.

suggested_fix.current: Blocker 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL' with no location
suggested_fix.proposed: Make the blocker a link that opens the modal and moves focus into the field, or surface the field on the canvas
